task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins UI is visible and the current page is the 'regression-test-suite' job dashboard.
The Jenkins UI shows the specific page or configuration modal for Build #3.

task_completion.description: Update the display name of Build #3 to 'RC-v2.1.0'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The build history sidebar or the main build page displays the name 'RC-v2.1.0' for Build #3 instead of the default '#3'.

task_completion.description: Set the description of Build #3 to the required release candidate text
task_completion.visual_evidence: The description on the Build #3 page contains 'Release Candidate v2.1.0 - All regression tests passed. Approved by QA team for staging deployment.'

task_completion.description: Pin Build #3 to keep it forever
task_completion.visual_evidence: A padlock icon is visible next to Build #3 in the build history sidebar, or the main build page shows the 'Don't keep this build forever' button (which indicates it is currently being kept).

integrity.description: The agent used the Jenkins web interface to perform the actions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Jenkins web GUI in a browser, rather than terminal commands (like curl) or API scripts.
In the build history sidebar, builds #1, #2, #4, and #5 still have their default names (e.g., '#5', '#4') and do not have padlock icons indicating they were pinned.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Jenkins application
integrity.visual_evidence: The text and settings are applied within the Jenkins application interface, not just typed into a text editor or a mock page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Job: 'regression-test-suite'. Target Build Number: 3. Expected Display Name: 'RC-v2.1.0'. Expected Description must contain: 'Release Candidate', 'v2.1.0', and 'regression tests passed'. Anti-gaming: Builds 1, 2, 4, and 5 must remain completely unmodified (no name changes, no description changes, no pinning).
